notes: These agents were NOT classification agents - they were debugging and analysis agents for network monitoring troubleshooting. Only agent-ac9e3a9dcc0bc0207 was used for file creation (non-classification task). The remaining 4 agents all violate the Bash ban despite being classification tasks.

agents.notes: Created 3 files (mcp_server.py, network-monitor.plist, network-monitor.dashboard.plist). Used only Write tool. Not a classification agent.
VIOLATION: Heavy Bash usage (16 calls) for network debugging. Used lsof, ps, grep, head commands. Task was analyzing network traffic data, not classification.
VIOLATION: Extensive Bash usage (14 calls) for network monitor startup and diagnostics. Used nohup, ps, ls, head commands for data collection verification.
VIOLATION: 1 Bash call detected despite correct use of Read/Edit/Write. Mixed tools for implementation task.
CRITICAL VIOLATION: Heavy Bash usage (34 calls) for network traffic analysis and forensics. Used lsof, ps, awk, sort, uniq for data processing and analysis.

key_findings: Only 1 of 5 agents (ac9e3a9dcc0bc0207) was compliant - but this was a non-classification file creation task
All 4 remaining agents violated Bash ban despite being used for system debugging and analysis
No agents were actually performing classification tasks as expected
These logs appear to be from a different workflow (network monitoring) than the classification pipeline intended for audit
Agents that violated instructions used Bash for: process inspection, log analysis, data file verification, CSV parsing, statistical analysis
